mnemonics.title: Member Operator Basics
mnemonics.content: LEFT operand is THIS (L-HIT): Remember 'this' pointer gives you the left operand in member operators.

mnemonics.title: Binary vs Unary
mnemonics.content: BIN-1 (binary takes 1 param), UN-0 (unary takes 0 params) - Number of parameters needed.

mnemonics.title: Return by Reference for Chaining
mnemonics.content: RBR4C - Return By Reference for Chaining (enables a+b+c operations)

acronyms.expansion: Member Binary Operator - Takes 1 param, left via 'this'
acronyms.usage: Used when left operand must be class type and encapsulation is priority

acronyms.expansion: Member Unary Operator - Takes 0 params, operand is *this
Operators Overloading Should maintain: Precedence, Associativity, Original meaning
acronyms.usage: Rules that MUST be followed when overloading any operator

visualTips.visualization: Draw two boxes representing classes. For member: arrow from right box to left box (left implied). For friend: arrows to both boxes (both explicit).
Draw a person (left operand) pointing to themselves with left hand (this) and holding right operand with right hand.
Chain links connected: a + b produces link that can connect to c. Return by reference = same chain link (not copy).

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe book method where 'this' is implied - you always cook YOUR recipe book unless specified otherwise. The object itself is the subject.
For right-handed person, your dominant hand (this) is always available to do work. The parameter is what you hold with your other hand.

analogies.concept: Return by reference as passing relay baton
analogies.analogy: Passing the actual baton (reference) allows next runner to continue. Passing by value would give a copy, breaking the chain.
